message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>To reconstruct the evolutionary dynamics of the 2019 novel coronavirus, 52 2019-nCOV genomes available on 04 February 2020 at GISAID were analysed.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The two models used to estimate the reproduction number (coalescent-based exponential growth and a birth-death skyline method) indicated an estimated mean evolutionary rate of 7.8 × 10
                  <jats:sup>−4</jats:sup>
                  subs/site/year (range 1.1×10
                  <jats:sup>−4</jats:sup>
                  –15×10
                  <jats:sup>−4</jats:sup>
                  ).
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>The estimated R value was 2.6 (range 2.1-5.1), and increased from 0.8 to 2.4 in December 2019. The estimated mean doubling time of the epidemic was between 3.6 and 4.1 days.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>This study proves the usefulness of phylogeny in supporting the surveillance of emerging new infections even as the epidemic is growing.</jats:p>
title: Early Phylogenetic Estimate Of The Effective Reproduction Number Of 2019-nCoV
